en: "Behold, my eye has seen all this, my ear has heard and understood it.
zh: “這一切我的眼睛都見過， 我的耳朵都聽過，而且明白。

en: What you know, I also know; I am not inferior to you.
zh: 你們所知道的我也知道， 我並非不及你們。

en: But I would speak to the Almighty, and I desire to argue my case with God.
zh: 但我要對全能者說話， 我願與 神辯論。

en: As for you, you whitewash with lies; worthless physicians are you all.
zh: 你們都是捏造謊言的， 都是無用的醫生。

en: Oh that you would keep silent, and it would be your wisdom!
zh: 但願你們完全不作聲， 這樣才算為你們的智慧。

en: Hear now my argument and listen to the pleadings of my lips.
zh: 請你們聽我的辯論， 留心聽我嘴唇的申訴。

en: Will you speak falsely for God and speak deceitfully for him?
zh: 你們要為 神說不義的言語嗎？ 你們要為他說詭詐的話嗎？

en: Will you show partiality toward him? Will you plead the case for God?
zh: 你們要徇 神的情面嗎？ 要為 神爭辯嗎？

en: Will it be well with you when he searches you out? Or can you deceive him, as one deceives a man?
zh: 他把你們查出來，這是好嗎？ 人怎樣哄騙人，你們也怎樣哄騙 神嗎？

en: He will surely rebuke you if in secret you show partiality.
zh: 你們若暗中徇情面， 他必然責備你們。

en: Will not his majesty terrify you, and the dread of him fall upon you?
zh: 他的尊嚴不是叫你們懼怕嗎？ 他的驚嚇不是臨到你們嗎？

en: Your maxims are proverbs of ashes; your defenses are defenses of clay.
zh: 你們背誦的格言都是爐灰的格言， 你們的辯護都是泥土的辯護。

en: "Let me have silence, and I will speak, and let come on me what may.
zh: 你們要靜默，離開我，好讓我說話， 然後不論甚麼事也好，讓它臨到我吧。

en: Why should I take my flesh in my teeth and put my life in my hand?
zh: 我已把我的肉掛在自己的牙上， 把我的命放在自己的手中。

en: Though he slay me, I will hope in him; yet I will argue my ways to his face.
zh: 他必殺我，我沒有指望了， 我必在他面前辯明我所行的；

en: This will be my salvation, that the godless shall not come before him.
zh: 這要成為我的拯救， 因為不敬虔的人不能到他面前來。

en: Keep listening to my words, and let my declaration be in your ears.
zh: 你們當細聽我的言語， 讓我的宣言進入你們的耳中。

en: Behold, I have prepared my case; I know that I shall be in the right.
zh: 現在我已呈上我的案件， 我知道我自己得算為義，

en: Who is there who will contend with me? For then I would be silent and die.
zh: 有誰與我相爭呢？ 若有，我就默然不言，氣絕而亡。

en: Only grant me two things, then I will not hide myself from your face:
zh: 只要不對我行兩件事， 我就不躲開你的面，

en: withdraw your hand far from me, and let not dread of you terrify me.
zh: 就是把你的手縮回，遠離我身， 又不使你的驚懼威嚇我。

en: Then call, and I will answer; or let me speak, and you reply to me.
zh: 這樣，你一呼叫，我就回答， 或是讓我說話，你回答我。

en: How many are my iniquities and my sins? Make me know my transgression and my sin.
zh: 我的罪孽與罪過有多少呢？ 求你讓我知道我的過犯與罪過。

en: Why do you hide your face and count me as your enemy?
zh: 你為甚麼掩面， 把我當作你的仇敵呢？

en: Will you frighten a driven leaf and pursue dry chaff?
zh: 你要使被風吹動的樹葉戰抖嗎？ 你要追趕枯乾了的碎稭嗎？

en: For you write bitter things against me and make me inherit the iniquities of my youth.
zh: 你記錄判詞攻擊我， 又使我承當我幼年的罪孽；

en: You put my feet in the stocks and watch all my paths; you set a limit for the soles of my feet.
zh: 你把我的兩腳上了木狗， 又鑒察我一切所行的， 為我的腳掌定界限。

en: Man wastes away like a rotten thing, like a garment that is moth-eaten.
zh: 我的生命像破滅腐朽之物， 又像蟲蛀的衣服。”
